title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in El Paso, Texas
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in El Paso? The truck's black box data can DISAPPEAR within days. Don't wait — this evidence could make or break your case. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) now. https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-el-paso-texas
linkedin:
If you or someone you know has been involved in a semi-truck crash in El Paso, Texas, there is one critical piece of evidence that most victims never think to protect: the truck's black box.

Commercial trucks are equipped with Event Data Recorders (EDRs) and Engine Control Modules (ECMs) that capture speed, braking, hours of service, and driver behavior in the moments before a crash. This data is legally admissible, highly persuasive, and — critically — it can be overwritten or destroyed within days if no legal hold is issued.

For attorneys in our network of 800+ law firms across the country, this is exactly the kind of time-sensitive evidence that separates a strong case from a difficult one. Preservation letters must go out immediately. Trucking companies have legal teams ready to act fast. Injured victims deserve representation that acts just as quickly.

WreckMatch was built to solve this problem. We connect injured truck accident victims in El Paso and across Texas with experienced, qualified attorneys who understand commercial trucking litigation — fast.

If you are an attorney handling truck accident cases in Texas, or a victim who needs help now, visit us at https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form to get connected today.
facebook:
If you were hit by a semi-truck in El Paso, you may not realize that the truck itself was recording everything.

Commercial trucks carry a black box — similar to what you find on airplanes — that logs speed, braking, engine data, and even whether the driver was complying with hours-of-service rules. After a crash, this data is one of the most powerful pieces of evidence available.

The problem? It can be deleted or overwritten in as little as 30 days. Some systems reset even faster.

At WreckMatch, we help injured victims in El Paso connect quickly with experienced truck accident attorneys who know how to send preservation letters, issue spoliation holds, and fight for the evidence you deserve before it disappears.

You should not have to navigate this alone while you are recovering. The trucking company already has a legal team working — you deserve one too.

Have you or someone you love ever been in a crash with a commercial truck and not known what steps to take first?
reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in El Paso — how do I preserve the black box data before it's gone?
reddit_body:
If you were recently in a crash involving a commercial semi-truck in El Paso, one of the most important things you can do right now is take steps to preserve the truck's black box data. Commercial trucks have Event Data Recorders (EDRs) and Engine Control Modules (ECMs) that log speed, hard braking, throttle position, and hours-of-service compliance. This data can be critical evidence — but it is not stored forever.

The biggest risk is that trucking companies are not required to preserve this data unless they receive a legal hold or spoliation letter. Some systems overwrite data within 30 days, and others even sooner depending on how the truck is used after the crash. If you wait too long to take action, that evidence may be gone permanently with no way to recover it.

The best move is to contact an attorney who handles commercial truck accidents as soon as possible. They can send a formal preservation letter to the trucking company and their insurer quickly. If you are not sure where to start or how to find an attorney experienced in trucking cases specifically, wreckmatch.com matches injured victims with qualified truck accident lawyers — it is free to use and built for exactly this kind of situation.
